description: SSO (SAML 2.0) integration to be delivered as part of Hartfield Group's enterprise contract renewal. Ravi Patel committed to this in the renewal negotiation call on October 14, 2024 as a condition of closing the renewal.
evidence_source: Salesforce opportunity note (opp-HG-2024-RNW), Ravi Patel email to Hartfield IT Director dated October 16, 2024
notes: James Okafor raised concerns about the June 2025 timeline during the initial scoping in November 2024 — he estimated 14 weeks of Engineering work. The commitment was made before Engineering was consulted. SSO is now blocked by the Q1 2026 tech debt freeze. Hartfield's account is marked 'at-risk' in Salesforce. Chloe Beaumont is managing the relationship. No revised delivery date has been communicated to Hartfield.

description: Custom reporting dashboard with configurable financial metrics views, to be delivered as part of Meridian Capital's account expansion (ARR uplift from £84K to £140K/year). Tom Adeyemi committed to this during the expansion negotiation.
evidence_source: Salesforce expansion opportunity (opp-MC-2025-EXP), Product spec in Notion (draft, not finalized)
notes: The spec was drafted in December 2025 but Linear tickets were not created before the Q1 2026 tech debt freeze was announced. Tom Adeyemi is now managing this manually — the dashboard is not scheduled for any sprint. March 31 deadline is at serious risk. Chloe Beaumont has not yet surfaced this risk to Meridian Capital.

description: CS team to deliver a consolidated, structured bug report template to Engineering, replacing the ad-hoc reports that were creating noise in the Engineering backlog. Priya Sharma committed to this in the Q3 2025 cross-functional planning meeting.
evidence_source: Q3 2025 planning meeting notes (Notion), Chloe Beaumont Notion template (published Jan 9, 2026)
notes: Chloe Beaumont delivered the consolidated bug report template on January 9, 2026 — 6 days ahead of deadline. Template is now live and being used. Engineering confirmed receipt and adoption in sprint planning on January 13, 2026. One of the few cross-functional commitments that was tracked and delivered on time.

description: Product team to publish a publicly accessible customer-facing roadmap by end of Q1 2026, giving CS the tool they need to answer 'what's coming' questions in renewal and QBR calls.
evidence_source: Q4 2025 planning meeting notes (Notion), Slack message from Tom Adeyemi to Priya Sharma, November 22, 2025
notes: The public roadmap was never published. Tom Adeyemi deprioritized it in January 2026 when the tech debt freeze was announced — he argued there was nothing meaningful to put on a roadmap during a feature freeze. Priya Sharma considers this broken. The relationship between CS and Product is strained as a result. Priya raised this directly with Fatima Al-Rashid in February 2026.

description: Dedicated Customer Success Manager assigned to Solaris Financial post-renewal — not a shared CSM, but a named account CSM. This was a key ask from Solaris's Head of Finance, who had experienced poor responsiveness from the shared CSM pool.
evidence_source: Salesforce renewal opportunity (opp-SF-2025-RNW), Contract addendum Section 4.2
notes: CSM assignment is pending a headcount decision. Priya Sharma has asked Fatima for approval to hire one additional CSM. The hire is in process (candidate screening stage) but has not closed. Solaris's contract went live January 1, 2026 — they are 60 days past renewal with no dedicated CSM assigned. Chloe Beaumont is covering the account temporarily.

description: Engineering to complete full test coverage for the Finance Workflow Engine module, bringing coverage from 22% to 70% by end of Q1 2026 as part of the tech debt freeze scope.
evidence_source: Q1 2026 Engineering kick-off doc (Notion), Linear project: 'FWE Test Coverage'
notes: This is on track as of the last sprint review (March 10, 2026). Current coverage at 58%. Dev Anand and James Okafor are the primary contributors. Target is 70% by March 31. Yuki has flagged that 70% is achievable but reaching edge cases in the reconciliation logic may push to 65%.

description: Finance to deliver a board-ready financial model with 3-year projections for Series C preparation by April 30, 2026.
evidence_source: 2025 Annual Planning session notes, direct conversation between Sarah Chen and Nadia Kovalchuk
notes: This is Nadia's top priority for Q1/Q2 2026. The model is being built in Google Sheets and will incorporate the AI pilot results from invoice reconciliation. Sarah needs it for investor conversations expected to start in May 2026. Kwame Asante (actor-020) is providing the underlying financial data.

description: CTO to present a technical roadmap and architecture modernization plan to the board by Q2 2026, following the Q1 2026 tech debt freeze. Board needs visibility into the state of the platform before approving Series C use-of-funds.
notes: Marcus Webb is working on this. The Q1 tech debt work is expected to provide the data points needed. Yuki Tanaka is contributing the engineering metrics section. Oliver Mwangi is contributing the squad structure and capacity planning section. First draft expected April 15, 2026.

description: Personal commitment from CEO Sarah Chen to Hartfield Group's CFO (Richard Hartfield) that SSO and a dedicated enterprise support SLA would be in place before Hartfield's next quarterly board review, which falls in July 2026.
evidence_source: Email from Sarah Chen to Richard Hartfield, March 2, 2026
notes: This is a higher-level reinforcement of com-001. Sarah made this commitment personally after a tense call with Hartfield's CFO in early March. Engineering is not aware of this escalated personal commitment from the CEO. Fatima Al-Rashid is the only other person who knows about it.

description: COO to approve the People Operations team's request for a new HRIS module (performance management) within the Q1 2026 budget cycle.
evidence_source: Slack thread between Fatima Al-Rashid and Anya Volkov, January 28, 2026
notes: Approved March 1, 2026. Rippling performance module to be activated Q2 2026. Anya Volkov's team will configure. On track.

description: Head of Sales committed to Meridian Capital that Veridian would support bulk CSV export of all financial reports by Q1 2026, as part of their contract negotiation.
evidence_source: Salesforce opportunity notes (opp-MC-2025-EXP), no Linear ticket found
notes: This commitment was made verbally by Ravi Patel and noted in Salesforce, but a Linear ticket was never created and Engineering was never briefed. Discovered by Chloe Beaumont during March 2026 QBR prep. Meridian Capital has not raised it yet — likely because they haven't needed the feature. Risk of discovery at renewal.

description: Platform Engineering to deliver a self-service SSO configuration interface (for Veridian admins, not customers) to reduce CS burden when provisioning customer workspaces.
evidence_source: Tech debt freeze project list, Linear project: 'Admin SSO Self-Serve'
notes: This is part of the Q1 2026 tech debt freeze scope and is directly related to the Hartfield customer commitment (com-001). James Okafor is leading. Timeline is Q2 2026 because Q1 is fully committed to foundational infrastructure work. This is a prerequisite for delivering SSO to Hartfield.

description: Oliver Mwangi promised Marcus Webb that all 3 engineering squads would have full runbook coverage completed by end of Q1 2026, as part of Oliver's mandate to bring Google-scale engineering practices to Veridian.
evidence_source: Engineering All-Hands notes, January 6, 2026; Slack message from Oliver Mwangi in #engineering-leadership, January 8, 2026
notes: Only Squad 1 achieved full runbook coverage by the Q1 deadline. Squads 2 and 3 are at approximately 45% and 30% coverage respectively. Oliver flagged this to Marcus in the March 28 leadership sync — coverage was blocked by the depth of tech debt in the underlying systems, which made documenting runbooks harder than expected. Marcus accepted the explanation but noted it in the formal performance context for Oliver's annual review.

description: Sophie Laurent committed to Sarah Chen that HubSpot-Salesforce attribution would be working end-to-end by end of Q2 2026, enabling marketing ROI measurement for the first time.
evidence_source: Q1 2026 Marketing review meeting notes, March 3, 2026; Slack message from Sophie Laurent to Sarah Chen, March 5, 2026
notes: Known blocker: Ben Osei (actor-015) owns the Intercom-Salesforce integration and would be the primary engineer on the HubSpot-Salesforce sync work. This work is not currently in any sprint or on any roadmap. Sophie has not formally escalated the dependency to Oliver Mwangi or Fatima. If June 30 is to be achievable, the work needs to be scoped and scheduled in Q2 2026 immediately.
